section: Chapter 3: How the State Government Works

questions.question: Who is an MLA?
questions.correct_answer: A Member of the Legislative Assembly elected by the people
questions.explanation: MLAs are elected from specific constituencies and represent the people in the state government.

questions.question: What is a 'Constituency'?
questions.correct_answer: A specific area from which all the voters living there choose one representative
questions.explanation: Every state in India is divided into different areas or constituencies based on population.

questions.question: How does an MLA become a Minister or Chief Minister?
questions.correct_answer: The political party with the majority selects their leader, who is then appointed by the Governor
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister then selects other MLAs to head various departments as Ministers.

questions.question: What is the 'Legislative Assembly'?
questions.correct_answer: A place where all MLAs meet to discuss and debate matters of the state
questions.explanation: It is the main body where laws are made and the executive is held accountable.
questions.source_section: A Debate in the Legislative Assembly

questions.question: What is meant by the 'Ruling Party'?
questions.correct_answer: The political party that has the majority of MLAs in the assembly
questions.explanation: Any party that wins more than half the number of constituencies in a state is called the majority/ruling party.

questions.question: Who is the 'Opposition'?
questions.correct_answer: Elected members who are not part of the ruling party
questions.explanation: The opposition plays a crucial role by questioning the government and suggesting different views.

questions.question: Who is the Head of the State?
questions.explanation: The Governor is appointed by the Central Government to ensure the state government works according to the Constitution.

questions.question: What are the two ways the government acts on issues?
questions.correct_answer: Through discussions in the Legislative Assembly and through Press Conferences
questions.explanation: The government explains its actions to the public via media and answers questions in the assembly.

questions.question: Who has the power to make laws on specific subjects for the state?
questions.correct_answer: The Legislative Assembly of the state
questions.explanation: While the assembly makes laws, the actual implementation is done by various government departments.

questions.question: What is a Press Conference?
questions.correct_answer: A gathering of journalists invited to hear about and ask questions on a particular issue
questions.explanation: It is a medium used by ministers to inform the public about the steps the government is taking.

notes.title: Executive vs. Legislature
notes.content: The 'Legislature' refers to all MLAs in the assembly; the 'Executive' is the small group of ministers headed by the Chief Minister.

notes.title: Dual Role of an MLA
notes.content: An MLA can have two responsibilities: one as a representative of their constituency and another as a Minister.
notes.source_section: A Debate in the Legislative Assembly

notes.title: Public Awareness
notes.content: Apart from the assembly, people express views through newspapers, TV channels, and protest rallies.

facts.fact: Himachal Pradesh is divided into 68 assembly constituencies.
The Governor is appointed to make sure the state follows the rules of the Indian Constitution.
The Chief Minister and other ministers have separate offices to run various government departments like Health or Education.
